context: Fearfully and wonderfully made
point_summary: God's power to help in impossible odds is grounded in who He is as Creator—the One who formed humanity fearfully and wonderfully can certainly overcome a million-man army.

context: Behold how good and how pleasant it is for brethren to dwell together in unity
point_summary: Restored unity among brothers is portrayed as genuinely good and pleasant, set against the pride-driven contention that strains relationships when being right eclipses being reconciled.

context: a statement here from Psalm 119
point_summary: Brief mention; no exposition in this segment.

context: we will not hide them from their children. Showing to the generation to come the praises of the Lord and his strength and his wonderful works that he hath done
point_summary: Parents bear a God-given responsibility to transmit knowledge of God's praises, strength, and works across generations, refusing to hide these truths from their children.

context: For he established a testimony in Jacob and appointed a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers that they should make them known to their children
point_summary: God's command in Psalm 78:5 — that fathers make His testimonies known to their children — is presented as the generational mandate underlying the parental responsibility to instruct and reprove.
